title: Make orchestration dependencies and SLAs explicit
description: Model task dependencies as a DAG with declared SLAs and alerting so a late or failed upstream is visible, not silently stale.
body: Use when scheduling multi-step pipelines. Do not chain jobs by guessing run times or cron offsets — a slow upstream then feeds a downstream stale or empty data. Actions: declare explicit task dependencies in the orchestrator, set SLAs/timeouts, trigger downstream on upstream success (data-aware scheduling), and alert on miss. Validation signals include a DAG where every downstream waits on its real upstream and an SLA-miss alert firing on delay. Risk boundary: a hidden timing dependency produces correct-looking but stale outputs; treat cron-coupled pipelines as fragile and convert them to explicit dependencies.
